title: Create and Maintain Global Objects
text: Create and Maintain Global Objects Create global objects such as global ProcScripts and messages in a runtime library. In the Uniface IDE, choose More Editors and select the type of global object (under the Library heading.) In the Open Library dialog, create or select the library to hold the objects. For more information, see Create a Library for Modeled Properties or Global Objects . To create a new object, click New . To update an existing object, select it in the object list. Set the object properties as required. Tip: Press F1 to display help about the properties of object. If the object requires compilation, click the compile icon (if available) or choose File>Compile . To save the object, choose File > Save . Alternatively, click OK to (compile and) save the object, and close the editor. To delete an object, select it and click Delete (if available) or choose Edit > Delete <Object> . Related Links Global Objects A global object is a reusable, technology-independent object that is centrally stored and available to all components running on a given host, regardless of the deployment environment.
